title: athyrium mcdonellii
intro: Athyrium mcdonellii is a species of fern native to the Himalayas, classified as a vascular plant belonging to the family Athyriaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with roots and rhizomes used to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Athyrium mcdonellii include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. In herbal preparations, Athyrium mcdonellii is often used in the form of dried roots and rhizomes, which can be made into decoctions or teas.
